entries.summary: Dependency graph analysis for Agent OS Windows port plan (5 EPICs). Result: EPIC-001 (modularize install.sh) has no deps. EPIC-002 (Windows installer) depends on EPIC-001 (needs extracted .py scripts and modular structure). EPIC-003 (ostwin.ps1 CLI) depends on EPIC-002 (needs Windows environment for testing). EPIC-004 (port 13 shell scripts) depends on EPIC-003 (needs ostwin.ps1 dispatcher). EPIC-005 (CI/CD) depends on EPIC-001 and EPIC-002 (needs both bash modules and PS1 installer to validate). Maximum parallelism: Wave 1=EPIC-001, Wave 2=EPIC-002, Wave 3=EPIC-003||EPIC-005 (parallel), Wave 4=EPIC-004.
entries.detail:
## Dependency Graph JSON

```json
{
  "EPIC-001": {"depends_on": []},
  "EPIC-002": {"depends_on": ["EPIC-001"]},
  "EPIC-003": {"depends_on": ["EPIC-002"]},
  "EPIC-004": {"depends_on": ["EPIC-003"]},
  "EPIC-005": {"depends_on": ["EPIC-001", "EPIC-002"]}
}
```

## Rationale

### EPIC-001 → no deps
Foundation epic. Works on existing monolithic install.sh. No prerequisites.

### EPIC-002 → EPIC-001
EPIC-002 DoD line 70: "MCP config patching reuses the extracted .py scripts from EPIC-001"
EPIC-002 mirrors the modular structure that EPIC-001 creates. Without modules, there's nothing to mirror.

### EPIC-003 → EPIC-002
All 6 ACs explicitly state depends_on: EPIC-002. The CLI needs:
- Windows installer to set up Python venv, Node, dashboard, MCP servers
- Cannot test "ostwin run executes plans" without working Windows installation

### EPIC-004 → EPIC-003
DoD line 124: "The ostwin.ps1 CLI (EPIC-003) dispatches to .ps1 versions"
All ACs test via `ostwin <command>` requiring EPIC-003's dispatcher.
Note: Code translation CAN start from bash scripts, but integration/validation needs EPIC-003.

### EPIC-005 → EPIC-001, EPIC-002
- Needs BATS tests for bash modules (EPIC-001 output)
- Needs Pester tests for PS1 installer (EPIC-002 output)
- AC line 147: "CI green on all 3 platforms for every PR touching .agents/"

## Parallelism Analysis
- Wave 1: EPIC-001 (solo)
- Wave 2: EPIC-002 (solo, blocked by Wave 1)
- Wave 3: EPIC-003 || EPIC-005 (parallel! both only need EPIC-002)
- Wave 4: EPIC-004 (blocked by EPIC-003)

Maximum parallelism achieved at Wave 3 where EPIC-003 and EPIC-005 run concurrently.

entries.summary: Modularized install.sh (EPIC-001): Decomposed 1926-line monolith into 17 shell modules + 4 Python scripts under .agents/installer/. install.sh is now a 144-line thin orchestrator that sources modules. Module structure: lib.sh (colors/formatting/ask/version_gte), versions.conf (MIN_PYTHON=3.10, NODE_VER=v25.8.1, PWSH=7.4.7), detect-os.sh, check-deps.sh, install-deps.sh, install-files.sh, setup-venv.sh, setup-env.sh, patch-mcp.sh, build-frontend.sh (unified build_frontend replacing build_nextjs+build_dashboard_fe), setup-path.sh, setup-opencode.sh, sync-agents.sh, start-dashboard.sh, start-channels.sh, verify.sh, _orchestrate-deps.sh. Python scripts: patch_opencode_permissions.py, inject_env_to_mcp.py, merge_mcp_builtin.py, merge_mcp_to_opencode.py. All CLI flags preserved: --yes, --dir, --dashboard-only, --channel, --source-dir, --port, --skip-optional, --help.
entries.detail:
## Module Map

### .agents/install.sh (144 lines — orchestrator)
- Argument parsing (--yes, --dir, --source-dir, --port, --skip-optional, --dashboard-only, --channel, --help)
- Sources all 16 modules from .agents/installer/
- Orchestration flow: detect OS → check/install deps → build frontends → install files → setup venv → patch MCP → setup env → opencode permissions → powershell modules → PATH → verify → start dashboard → channels → banner

### .agents/installer/ (16 shell modules + 1 config)
```
lib.sh              — Colors, formatting, ask(), version_gte()
versions.conf       — MIN_PYTHON_VERSION=3.10, PYTHON_INSTALL_VERSION=3.12, MIN_PWSH_VERSION=7, PWSH_INSTALL_VERSION=7.4.7, NODE_VER=v25.8.1
detect-os.sh        — detect_os() → OS, DISTRO, PKG_MGR, ARCH
check-deps.sh       — check_python(), check_pwsh(), check_node(), check_uv(), check_opencode(), check_brew()
install-deps.sh     — install_brew(), install_uv(), install_python(), install_pwsh(), install_node(), install_opencode(), install_pester()
install-files.sh    — install_files(), compute_build_hash(), internal: _seed_mcp_config, _sync_amem, _setup_mcp_symlink, _migrate_mcp_config, _sync_dashboard, _load_contributed_roles
setup-venv.sh       — setup_venv()
setup-env.sh        — setup_env(), internal: _create_env_sh_hook, _migrate_env_keys
patch-mcp.sh        — patch_mcp_config()
build-frontend.sh   — build_frontend(subdir, label) — UNIFIED from build_nextjs + build_dashboard_fe
setup-path.sh       — setup_path()
setup-opencode.sh   — setup_opencode_permissions()
sync-agents.sh      — sync_opencode_agents()
start-dashboard.sh  — start_dashboard(), publish_skills(), internal: _check_tunnel
start-channels.sh   — install_channels(), start_channels()
verify.sh           — verify_components(), print_completion_banner()
_orchestrate-deps.sh — dependency check/install orchestration (step 2, handles --dashboard-only branching)
```

### .agents/installer/scripts/ (4 Python scripts)
```
patch_opencode_permissions.py — Patches ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json permissions
inject_env_to_mcp.py         — Injects .env vars into MCP server environment blocks
merge_mcp_builtin.py          — Merges built-in MCP servers into config.json
merge_mcp_to_opencode.py      — Normalizes, validates, merges MCP into opencode.json
```

### Key Design Decisions
1. Each module has a double-source guard ([[ -n "${_MODULE_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0)
2. No module has side effects when sourced — only function definitions
3. build_frontend() is parameterized: build_frontend "dashboard/nextjs" "Next.js dashboard"
4. Python heredocs extracted to standalone .py files with CLI interfaces (sys.argv)
5. Shellcheck passes clean on all 18 shell files
6. 70 BATS tests across 16 test files, all passing

entries.summary: EPIC-001 Design Decision: Module sourcing pattern with guards. Each module uses [[ -n "${_MODULE_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0 to prevent double-sourcing. install.sh loops through all modules to source them. Dependency orchestration (_orchestrate-deps.sh) is inline-sourced because it contains conditional flow (if/else for --dashboard-only) that modifies global state rather than defining functions.

EPIC-001 extracted Python scripts API: (1) patch_opencode_permissions.py <config_path> — patches OpenCode permissions for .env reads. (2) inject_env_to_mcp.py <mcp_config> <env_file> — injects .env vars into MCP servers. (3) merge_mcp_builtin.py <config> <builtin> — merges built-in servers into config. (4) merge_mcp_to_opencode.py <mcp_source> <opencode_file> <mcp_module_dir> — normalizes/validates/merges MCP into opencode.json (imports validate_mcp from mcp_module_dir).

EPIC-001 Convention: versions.conf is the single source of truth for version constants. MIN_PYTHON_VERSION=3.10, PYTHON_INSTALL_VERSION=3.12, MIN_PWSH_VERSION=7, PWSH_INSTALL_VERSION=7.4.7, NODE_VER=v25.8.1. All modules source versions.conf — no hard-coded version strings anywhere else.

EPIC-002 install.ps1 CLI interface: .\install.ps1 [-Yes] [-Dir path] [-SourceDir path] [-Port 9000] [-DashboardOnly] [-Channel] [-SkipOptional] [-Help]. Mirrors install.sh --yes/--dir/--source-dir/--port/--dashboard-only/--channel/--skip-optional/--help flags exactly. Default install dir: $HOME\.ostwin. Requires Windows 10/11, PowerShell 5.1+ (7+ recommended). No WSL/Cygwin/Git Bash dependency.

EPIC-002 TASK-012: Fixed TestHelper.ps1 Pester test scoping bug. The Import-InstallerModule function now writes a combined temp script via New-InstallerModuleScript, and test files dot-source the result with `. $script:_ImportedModuleScript`. This fixes PowerShell's scope limitation where dot-sourcing inside a function doesn't expose functions to the caller's scope. Also fixed cross-platform resilience: Patch-MCP.ps1, Setup-OpenCode.ps1, Sync-Agents.ps1 now fall back to $HOME when $env:USERPROFILE is null (macOS/Linux). Setup-Env.Tests.ps1 isolates from host API keys during placeholder test. Result: 144 pass, 0 fail, 6 skip (Windows-only) on macOS. All 34 .ps1 files parse cleanly.
entries.detail:
## Files Modified

### TestHelper.ps1 (.agents/installer/tests/windows/TestHelper.ps1)
- Replaced `Import-InstallerModule` implementation: now stores combined temp script path in `$script:_ImportedModuleScript`
- Added `New-InstallerModuleScript` function that returns the temp file path
- Uses `[ScriptBlock]::Create()` regex to strip both guard check and guard-set lines
- Test files dot-source `$script:_ImportedModuleScript` after calling Import-InstallerModule

### 16 Test Files Updated (all .agents/installer/tests/windows/*.Tests.ps1)
- Added `. $script:_ImportedModuleScript` after every `Import-InstallerModule` call
- Setup-Env.Tests.ps1: added env isolation for "Should contain provider key placeholders" test

### 3 Module Files Fixed for Cross-Platform Resilience
- Patch-MCP.ps1: added `$HOME` fallback when `$env:USERPROFILE` is null
- Setup-OpenCode.ps1: added `$HOME` fallback when `$env:USERPROFILE` is null
- Sync-Agents.ps1: added `$HOME` fallback when `$env:USERPROFILE` is null

## Root Cause Analysis
PowerShell scoping: when you dot-source a file inside a function, the functions defined in that file are only visible within the function's scope, not the caller's scope. The fix is to have the function return a temp file path and let the caller (BeforeAll block) dot-source it directly.

entries.summary: EPIC-002 Pester test convention: All test files must follow this pattern: (1) BeforeAll: dot-source TestHelper.ps1, call Import-InstallerModule, then dot-source $script:_ImportedModuleScript. (2) Tests using env vars must isolate from host environment (save/restore pattern). (3) Windows-only tests use -Skip with $IsWindows check. (4) Module files referencing $env:USERPROFILE must include $HOME fallback for cross-platform testing.

EPIC-005: Created .github/workflows/ci.yml — unified cross-platform CI/CD workflow for Agent OS. 6 jobs: (1) bats-installer-tests on ubuntu+macos (16 BATS test files, 70 tests for EPIC-001 bash modules), (2) pester-installer-tests on all 3 platforms (15 Pester test files, 144+ tests for EPIC-002 PS1 modules), (3) pester-core-tests on all 3 platforms (67 existing Pester tests from .agents/tests/ — plan, channel, dynamic_roles, lib, lifecycle, roles, war-rooms, mcp, observe, standalone), (4) smoke-test-bash on Linux (install.sh --yes --skip-optional + dashboard health check), (5) smoke-test-powershell on Windows (install.ps1 -Yes -SkipOptional + dashboard health check), (6) ci-gate — synthetic gate job that fails if any of the 5 other jobs fail. All jobs have 15-min timeout. JUnit XML results uploaded as artifacts (30-day retention). Test reports via dorny/test-reporter@v1. Triggers on push/PR touching .agents/** or the workflow itself. Branch protection: require 'CI Gate — All Checks' status check.
entries.detail:
## File Created
### .github/workflows/ci.yml (426 lines)

## Architecture
6 parallel jobs → 1 gate job:
```
bats-installer-tests (ubuntu, macos)     ─┐
pester-installer-tests (ubuntu, macos, windows) ─┤
pester-core-tests (ubuntu, macos, windows)      ─┼→ ci-gate
smoke-test-bash (ubuntu)                         ─┤
smoke-test-powershell (windows)                  ─┘
```

## Job Details

### 1. bats-installer-tests
- Matrix: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]
- Installs BATS (apt on Linux, brew on macOS)
- Runs: .agents/installer/tests/test_*.bats (16 files, 70 tests)
- Output: JUnit XML via `bats --formatter junit`

### 2. pester-installer-tests
- Matrix: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
- Runs: .agents/installer/tests/windows/ (15 files, 144+ tests)
- Windows-only tests auto-skipped on macOS/Linux via -Skip flag

### 3. pester-core-tests
- Matrix: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
- Runs 5 separate Invoke-Pester calls:
  1. Plan tests (isolated — .agents/tests/plan/)
  2. Core tests (channel, dynamic_roles, lib, lifecycle, roles, war-rooms)
  3. Standalone tests (.agents/tests/*.Tests.ps1)
  4. MCP tests (.agents/tests/mcp/)
  5. Observe tests (.agents/tests/observe/)

### 4. smoke-test-bash
- Runner: ubuntu-latest
- Runs: .agents/install.sh --yes --skip-optional
- Dashboard health check: imports dashboard.api FastAPI app, verifies routes > 0
- Fallback: ast.parse() syntax check if venv not available

### 5. smoke-test-powershell
- Runner: windows-latest
- Runs: .agents\install.ps1 -Yes -SkipOptional -SourceDir $PWD
- Dashboard health check: same pattern as bash, adapted for Windows paths

### 6. ci-gate
- Depends on all 5 jobs above
- if: always() — runs even if upstream jobs fail
- Fails (exit 1) if any upstream job has result == "failure"
- Branch protection: require this single check → blocks merge on failure

## Triggers
- push to: main, hotfix/*, feature/*
- pull_request to: main
- workflow_dispatch (manual)
- Path filter: .agents/** and .github/workflows/ci.yml

## Artifacts
- 5 upload-artifact steps (bats×2, pester-installer×3, pester-core×3, smoke-bash×1, smoke-ps1×1)
- 3 test-reporter steps with fail-on-error: true
- 30-day retention

## Relationship to Existing Workflow
- pester-tests.yml (existing) runs only on ubuntu-latest with core tests
- ci.yml (new) supersedes it functionally — runs core tests on all 3 platforms
- pester-tests.yml left in place to avoid breaking existing branch protections
- Recommended: after ci.yml is validated, update branch protection to require "CI Gate — All Checks" and disable the old pester-tests.yml check

entries.summary: EPIC-005 Decision: Single ci-gate job pattern for branch protection. Instead of requiring multiple matrix jobs as required status checks (which is fragile when matrix dimensions change), we use a single 'CI Gate — All Checks' job that depends on all upstream jobs and fails if any failed. Branch protection only needs to require this one check. This pattern scales as new jobs are added — just add to the ci-gate needs list.

EPIC-005 Convention: CI test isolation. Plan tests run in a separate Invoke-Pester call before other tests to prevent cross-test module pollution (same pattern as existing pester-tests.yml). Standalone root-level tests, MCP tests, and observe tests each run in their own Invoke-Pester call with conditional directory checks. All results written to separate JUnit XML files in test-results/.

EPIC-003: Created ostwin.ps1 — complete PowerShell port of the 1291-line bash ostwin CLI. Located at .agents/bin/ostwin.ps1 (~950 lines). Provides full command parity with 25+ subcommands: agent, run, plan (create/start/list/clear), init, sync, status, logs, stop, dashboard, channel, clone-role, skills (sync/install/search/update/remove/list), mcp, reload-env, role, mac, config, health, test, test-ps, version. Key Windows adaptations: (1) Import-EnvFile replaces bash set -a/source, (2) Invoke-RestMethod replaces curl, (3) Start-Process/Stop-Process/Get-Process replace nohup/kill/lsof, (4) Join-Path replaces path concatenation, (5) $env:USERPROFILE/$HOME replaces $HOME, (6) ConvertTo-Json/ConvertFrom-Json replaces python -c json, (7) No ps_dispatch() dual-dispatch — native .ps1 invocation. Also created ostwin.cmd wrapper for cmd.exe/Windows Terminal invocation. 68 Pester tests, all passing.
entries.detail:
## ostwin.ps1 Architecture

### Entry Point
```
.agents/bin/ostwin.ps1
```
Uses [CmdletBinding()] with $Command and $Arguments parameters. Main dispatch is a PowerShell switch statement.

### Helper Functions
- **Import-EnvFile** — Loads .env files, strips quotes, respects existing env vars
- **Test-DashboardReachable** — Health check via Invoke-RestMethod /api/status
- **Invoke-DashboardApi** — Generic dashboard API caller with auth headers
- **Resolve-PlanId** — Resolves hex plan IDs via API or file paths
- **Show-OstwinHelp** — Full help text matching bash version

### Flag Translation (bash → PS)
```
--dry-run     → -DryRun
--resume      → -Resume
--expand      → -Expand
--review      → -Review
--working-dir → -ProjectDir
--plan-file   → -PlanFile
--json        → -JsonOutput
--watch       → -Watch
--max-rooms   → -MaxConcurrent
--non-interactive → -NonInteractive
```

### Process Management
- Dashboard: PID file at $OstwinHome/dashboard.pid, Stop-Process for shutdown
- Channels: PID file at $OstwinHome/channels.pid
- stop command: graceful → force after 5s timeout

### GitHub Skills Install
Full port of the multi-skill repo scanner:
1. git clone --depth 1
2. Scan for SKILL.md in source/skills, .agents/skills, skills/, root
3. Detect nested vs top-level skills
4. Install to both ~/.ostwin/.agents/skills/ and ~/.ostwin/skills/
5. Write origin.json with provenance
6. Optional --agent flag for role-scoped install

### ostwin.cmd
```cmd
@echo off
where pwsh >nul 2>&1
if %errorlevel% equ 0 (
    pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "%~dp0ostwin.ps1" %*
    exit /b %errorlevel%
)
where powershell >nul 2>&1
if %errorlevel% equ 0 (
    powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "%~dp0ostwin.ps1" %*
    exit /b %errorlevel%
)
echo [ERROR] PowerShell not found.
exit /b 1
```

## Files Created
1. .agents/bin/ostwin.ps1 — Main CLI (~950 lines)
2. .agents/bin/ostwin.cmd — CMD wrapper (~20 lines)
3. .agents/bin/tests/ostwin.Tests.ps1 — Pester tests (68 tests)

entries.summary: EPIC-003 ostwin.ps1 CLI interface: ostwin.ps1 <command> [arguments]. Commands: agent, run, plan (create/start/list/clear), init, sync, status (--json/--watch), logs, stop (--force), dashboard (--port/--project-dir), channel, clone-role (--project-dir), skills (sync/install/search/update/remove/list with --agent/--from), mcp, reload-env, role (list/dispatch), mac (shorthand for macos-automation-engineer), config (--get/--set), health, test, test-ps, version. ostwin.cmd wrapper for cmd.exe invocation. Uses Invoke-RestMethod for API, Start-Process/Stop-Process for process management, Join-Path for paths.

EPIC-003 Convention: ostwin.ps1 translates bash-style flags to PowerShell parameters at dispatch time. Pattern: --dry-run → -DryRun, --working-dir → -ProjectDir, --json → -JsonOutput, --watch → -Watch, etc. No dual-dispatch/ps_dispatch() — all .ps1 scripts invoked directly via pwsh -NoProfile -File. PID files stored at $OstwinHome/dashboard.pid and $OstwinHome/channels.pid using Windows-compatible paths.

EPIC-004: Created 13 PowerShell ports of lifecycle shell scripts. Files created: .agents/init.ps1 (~180 lines), .agents/dashboard.ps1 (~155 lines), .agents/stop.ps1 (~120 lines), .agents/health.ps1 (~185 lines), .agents/sync.ps1 (~180 lines), .agents/sync-skills.ps1 (~225 lines), .agents/logs.ps1 (~145 lines), .agents/config.ps1 (~145 lines), .agents/uninstall.ps1 (~95 lines), .agents/memory-monitor.ps1 (~120 lines), .agents/clawhub-install.ps1 (~450 lines), .agents/bin/agent.ps1 (~70 lines), .agents/bin/memory.ps1 (~245 lines). Total: ~2,315 lines of PowerShell. All scripts parse cleanly. Updated .agents/bin/ostwin.ps1 dispatcher to prefer .ps1 over .sh for: init, sync, logs, config, health, dashboard, skills sync/install/search/update/remove. Bash fallback preserved for non-Windows. Created .agents/tests/lifecycle-scripts/LifecycleScripts.Tests.ps1 with 54 Pester tests — all passing.
entries.detail:
## Files Created

### Lifecycle Scripts (.agents/)
1. **init.ps1** — MCP initialization, scaffolds .agents/mcp, seeds config, updates .gitignore
2. **dashboard.ps1** — FastAPI dashboard launcher with -Port/-ProjectDir/-Background params
3. **stop.ps1** — Graceful shutdown with -Force flag, stops manager/dashboard/channels
4. **health.ps1** — Health check with -JsonOutput flag, checks manager/rooms/agents
5. **sync.ps1** — Framework sync preserving plans/.env/config.json, NTFS-safe paths
6. **sync-skills.ps1** — SKILL.md scanner with -InstallFrom/-Port/-Home params, dashboard API registration
7. **logs.ps1** — JSONL channel log viewer with -Follow/-Type/-From/-Last params
8. **config.ps1** — Config manager with -Get KEY / -Set KEY -Value VALUE, dot-notation navigation
9. **uninstall.ps1** — Clean removal with -Yes/-Dir params, Windows PATH cleanup via registry
10. **memory-monitor.ps1** — status/watch/on/off subcommands for memory venv management
11. **clawhub-install.ps1** — ClawHub registry client: install/search/update/remove/list

### CLI Scripts (.agents/bin/)
12. **agent.ps1** — opencode wrapper with .env loading and PID registration
13. **memory.ps1** — Memory CLI with publish/query/search/context/list subcommands

### Dispatcher Updates (.agents/bin/ostwin.ps1)
- init → prefers init.ps1 with -Yes flag translation
- sync → prefers sync.ps1
- logs → prefers logs.ps1 with -Follow/-Type/-From/-Last translation
- config → prefers config.ps1 with -Get/-Set/-Value translation
- health → prefers health.ps1 with -JsonOutput translation
- dashboard → prefers dashboard.ps1 with -Port/-ProjectDir/-Background translation
- skills sync → prefers sync-skills.ps1
- skills install/search/update/remove → prefers clawhub-install.ps1
- run init → prefers init.ps1 for working directory initialization

### Tests (.agents/tests/lifecycle-scripts/)
- LifecycleScripts.Tests.ps1 — 54 Pester tests covering:
  - Parse validation for all 13 scripts (13 tests)
  - init.ps1 functional tests: MCP scaffolding, .gitignore creation (2 tests)
  - config.ps1 functional tests: print/get/set modes (3 tests)
  - health.ps1 functional tests: JSON output, status reporting (2 tests)
  - logs.ps1 functional tests: display/filter/limit (3 tests)
  - stop.ps1 structural tests: parameter/function validation (2 tests)
  - memory-monitor.ps1 functional test: status output (1 test)
  - uninstall.ps1 functional test: missing dir handling (1 test)
  - sync.ps1 functional test: missing .agents error (1 test)
  - clawhub-install.ps1 functional tests: help, empty list (2 tests)
  - bin/agent.ps1 structural test: parameter structure (1 test)
  - bin/memory.ps1 functional tests: help text, parameter validation (2 tests)
  - ostwin.ps1 dispatch tests: verify all 8 commands prefer .ps1 (8 tests)
  - Bash non-regression tests: verify all 13 .sh files still exist (13 tests)

## Key Windows Adaptations
- Join-Path for all path construction (NTFS-safe)
- $env:USERPROFILE with $HOME fallback for cross-platform
- Start-Process/Stop-Process/Get-Process for process management
- Get-Content -Wait for tail-follow mode
- [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable for env management
- Invoke-RestMethod/Invoke-WebRequest for HTTP calls
- ConvertTo-Json/ConvertFrom-Json for JSON handling
- Expand-Archive for zip extraction
- Windows User PATH cleanup via registry in uninstall

entries.summary: EPIC-004 PowerShell script interfaces: init.ps1 [-TargetDir .] [-Yes] [-Help] | dashboard.ps1 [-Port 9000] [-ProjectDir pwd] [-Background] [-Help] | stop.ps1 [-Force] | health.ps1 [-JsonOutput] | sync.ps1 [-TargetDir .] | sync-skills.ps1 [-InstallFrom dir] [-Port 9000] [-Home dir] | logs.ps1 [-RoomId id] [-Follow] [-Type type] [-From role] [-Last N] | config.ps1 [-Get KEY] [-Set KEY -Value VAL] [-Help] | uninstall.ps1 [-Yes] [-Dir path] | memory-monitor.ps1 [-Command status|watch|on|off] | clawhub-install.ps1 <install|search|update|remove|list> [args] | bin/agent.ps1 [args...] | bin/memory.ps1 <publish|query|search|context|list|help> [args]

EPIC-004 Convention: ostwin.ps1 dispatcher prefers .ps1 scripts over .sh for all lifecycle commands. Pattern: check for scriptname.ps1 → dispatch via `pwsh -NoProfile -File` with flag translation; fallback to bash scriptname.sh if .ps1 not found. Flag translation: --yes → -Yes, --json → -JsonOutput, --follow → -Follow, --type → -Type, --from → -From, --last → -Last, --get → -Get, --set → -Set (with added -Value), --port → -Port, --project-dir → -ProjectDir, --background → -Background, --force → -Force, --install-from → -InstallFrom. For skills commands, both sync-skills and clawhub-install check .ps1 first, then .sh. All .ps1 scripts use [CmdletBinding()] with named parameters.
